pi_items.source: ImageJ/Fiji built-in sample image, accessible via File > Open Samples > Blobs (25K)
Web search confirmed: Blobs sample has dimensions 256 pixels width x 254 pixels height. Also verified in export_result.sh line 70 which checks: img.width == 256
Web search confirmed: Blobs sample has dimensions 256 pixels width x 254 pixels height. Also verified in export_result.sh line 70 which checks: img.height == 254
Matches path configuration in setup_task.sh (RESULTS_DIR) and is used in export_result.sh for image analysis. This is the configured output location.
Matches task description step 7 and is used in export_result.sh for CSV analysis. This is the configured output location.

Task description recommends 'radius ~40-60'. Web search on flat-field correction indicates for 256-pixel images, blur radius should be ~1/10 to 1/20 of image size (12.8-25.6 pixels). The value 20 is reasonable. However, the verifier.py does NOT check or enforce this value - it only verifies the output quality. The value appears to be metadata that isn't actively used in verification.

Task description explicitly states in step 2 and 5: 'Measure the mean intensity in 4 quadrants (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right)' and CSV report format requires 'Quadrant' column implying 4 quadrants.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the Blobs (25K) image, a built-in synthetic sample image in ImageJ/Fiji with verified dimensions of 256x254 pixels. The task requires measuring intensity in 4 quadrants (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right) before and after flat-field correction. Output files are configured to be saved at /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/flatfield_corrected.tif (image) and /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/illumination_report.csv (report). The data is synthetic, not from a real microscopy dataset.
